title: Member Functions
description: Give your objects the ability to take actions.

slides.title: Adding Verbs 🏃
slides.content:
## Making things move

Properties are the "Nouns" (What an object IS / what it HAS).
But objects also need "Verbs" (What an object DOES).

A Car has a `speed` (Property), but it also needs to `drive()` or `brake()` (Actions).
Functions defined inside a class are called **Member Functions** (or Methods).

slides.title: Creating Methods 🛠️
slides.content: To add an action, simply write a standard `fun` inside the class braces. These functions have direct access to internal properties!

quizData.question: What happens to the `fido` object's energy property every time you call `fido.bark()`?
quizData.explanation: Because `bark()` is internal to the class, it can directly permanently modify the object's properties (like `energy -= 1`).

slides.title: Scope Isolation 🛡️
slides.content:
## Independent Actors

If you have two Dog objects, `fido` and `rover`, causing Fido to bark only decreases Fido's energy. 

Rover's energy remains completely untouched. Objects operate entirely independently from one another!

quizData.question: If you call `fido.bark()`, which object's internal properties are modified?

options.text: Every Dog object in the entire app.

quizData.explanation: Member functions act exclusively upon the specific Instance (Object) that called them.
